data.title: Improve ergo show for epics
data.body:
## Scope
Restructure `ergo show <epic-id>` human output to be document-first: body upfront, task list as a compact summary section, metadata in a dim footer. Also fix subtask sort order to match `ergo list` (dependency order, not ID order).

## Non-goals
- No new flags or subcommands
- No changes to `ergo show <task-id>` (task show stays as-is)
- No changes to JSON output

## Constraints
- Reuse existing visual language: icons, colors, blocker annotations from tree_view.go
- The task list section in show output should feel consistent with list output

data.title: Fix subtask sort order in ergo show <epic>
data.body:
## Goal
- Fix `collectEpicChildren` to sort by dependency order (using `topoSortTasks`) instead of lexicographic ID order, so `ergo show <epic>` subtask order matches `ergo list`.

## Context
- `buildEpicTree` in `tree_view.go` already does this correctly (line 122: `tasks = topoSortTasks(tasks, graph)`).
- `collectEpicChildren` in `commands_work.go:914` sorts by ID only (`sort.Slice` on `children[i].ID < children[j].ID`).
- Replace that sort with `topoSortTasks(children, graph)` — graph is already available at call sites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When tasks in an epic have sequence edges (A→B→C), `ergo show <epic>` lists them in A, B, C order.
- Tasks with no dependency edges still appear (stable fallback to ID order via topoSort).

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...` passes
- Manual: create an epic with 3 tasks sequenced C→B→A, verify `show` orders them A, B, C

data.title: Redesign ergo show <epic> to be document-first
data.body:
## Goal
- Redesign `ergo show <epic-id>` human output so the body (the plan document) is the star, with a compact task list section below it and metadata in a dim footer.
- Task show output (`ergo show <task-id>`) is unchanged.

## New Layout
```
# Epic Title
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<body, glamour-rendered — front and center>

Tasks  ·  2 done · 1 doing · 2 todo
  ✓ Set up CI pipeline                            AAAAAA
  ✓ Add auth middleware                           BBBBBB
  ◐ Fix rate limiting  @agent-x                  CCCCCC
  ○ Write docs                                    DDDDDD
  · Deploy to prod  ⧗ Write docs                 EEEEEE
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
EPICID  ·  created 3 days ago  ·  updated 1 hour ago
```

## Context
- Current layout: metadata-heavy header → separator → body. For epics, each child task then gets a full `printTaskDetails` call, repeating metadata noise for every child.
- The task list compact rows should reuse `stateIcon`, `stateColor`, and blocker annotation logic from tree_view.go. Right-align IDs (same layout contract as list).
- If body is empty, omit the body section (just show task list + footer).
- The stats summary line (`2 done · 1 doing · 2 todo`) should reuse `computeStatsForTasks` + `renderSummary` or equivalent.
- Subtasks arrive already dependency-sorted from the fix in the preceding task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ergo show <epic-id>` renders: title heading → body → task list → footer
- Task list is compact (one line per task), not full printTaskDetails per task
- Shows claim info and blocker hints inline on each task row
- Footer shows epic ID + relative timestamps (dim)
- `ergo show <task-id>` output is unchanged
- JSON output (`ergo show --json <epic-id>`) is unchanged

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...` passes
- Manual: `ergo show <epic-id>` on an epic with body + mixed-state tasks looks clean

## Goal
- Make `ergo show <epic-id>` the fastest way for humans to understand an epic by leading with the plan document and a concise task status section.
- Ensure epic child ordering in `show` matches dependency order used by `list`, removing command-to-command ordering drift.

## Background / Rationale
- Current epic show output repeats full task metadata blocks per child, burying the epic narrative and increasing scan time.
- Child tasks are sorted lexicographically in `collectEpicChildren`, which can contradict dependency ordering shown elsewhere and confuse execution order.
- This epic aligns human output across commands while preserving existing machine contracts.

## Scope
- Human-mode rendering changes for `ergo show <epic-id>` only.
- Child ordering change from ID sort to dependency/topological order with deterministic fallback.
- Add regression coverage for ordering and epic/task show-output boundaries.

## Non-goals
- No new flags or subcommands.
- No behavior or layout changes for `ergo show <task-id>`.
- No changes to JSON schema/keys for `ergo show --json`.

## Constraints
- Reuse existing icons/colors/blocker hint behavior from `tree_view.go`.
- Keep ID alignment contract consistent with existing list output.
- Preserve current coding-agent hint behavior and existing `show` flag semantics.

## Definition of Done
- Both child tasks complete with passing automated gates.
- `ergo show <epic-id>` is document-first and compact for mixed-state epics.
- Dependency-ordered children are consistent across human and JSON show output.

## Goal
- Make `collectEpicChildren` return epic tasks in dependency order so `ergo show <epic-id>` task order matches `ergo list`.

## Background / Rationale
- `tree_view.go` already relies on `topoSortTasks`; `collectEpicChildren` still uses lexicographic ID sort.
- Inconsistent ordering across commands creates operator confusion and can encourage incorrect execution sequence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- For sequenced tasks A→B→C within one epic, both `ergo show <epic-id>` (human) and `ergo show --json <epic-id>` list children as A, B, C.
- Tasks in the epic without dependency edges still appear exactly once.
- When multiple tasks share the same dependency depth, fallback order is deterministic (ID).
- Existing `ergo show <task-id>` behavior is unchanged.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run 'TestCollectEpicChildrenSortsByDependencyOrder'`
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run 'TestShowEpicChildrenDependencyOrder'`
- `go test ./...`

## Goal
- Redesign human `ergo show <epic-id>` output to be document-first: epic body first, compact task list second, dim metadata footer last.
- Keep `ergo show <task-id>` output unchanged.

## Background / Rationale
- Current epic show prints full task detail blocks for each child, which overwhelms the epic document and slows status scanning.
- A compact task list preserves execution context while keeping the plan document as the primary artifact.

## Layout Contract
```
# Epic Title
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<body, glamour-rendered when TTY>

Tasks  ·  <summary counts>
  <icon/state> <title + inline claim/blocker hints> ... <ID right-aligned>
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<EPICID>  ·  created <relative>  ·  updated <relative>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Order is: title heading → body (if non-empty) → compact task list → dim footer.
- Task list uses one row per child (no nested `printTaskDetails` output).
- Rows include existing state icon/color semantics plus inline claim and blocker hints.
- Summary counts are derived from existing stats helpers (or exact equivalent behavior).
- IDs remain right-aligned to match list output conventions.
- Empty epic body omits the body section cleanly (no extra blank artifact).
- `ergo show <task-id>` output remains unchanged.
- `ergo show --json <epic-id>` output remains unchanged.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run 'TestShowEpicHumanDocumentFirstLayout|TestShowEpicOmitsBodySectionWhenEmpty|TestShowTaskHumanOutputUnchanged'`
- `go test ./...`
- Manual: run `ergo show <epic-id>` against a mixed-state epic in both TTY and non-TTY contexts.

## Consult Me
- If preserving right-aligned IDs requires truncation/wrapping tradeoffs for long titles, pause and confirm the preferred behavior before finalizing.
- If you need to change glyphs, color semantics, or footer wording beyond this contract, confirm before merging.

data.title: Add body to ergo list --json output
data.body:
## Goal
- Add `body` to the `taskListItem` JSON output so agents can get full task content from `ergo list --json` in a single call, without looping through `ergo show` per task.

## Context
- `taskListItem` in `output.go:16` omits `body`; `taskShowOutput` includes it.
- Agents in the wild work around this by looping: `for id in ...; do ergo --json show $id | python3 -c "...extract title, body..."; done`
- Fix is a one-field addition to `taskListItem` and `buildTaskListItems`.
- If help.txt or quickstart.txt document the list JSON schema, update them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ergo --json list` output includes `"body": "..."` on each task/epic item (empty string when no body).
- The 8-command loop pattern is fully replaceable with a single `ergo list --json` call.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...` passes
- `ergo --json list | jq ".[].body"` emits values (or empty strings) for all items

## Goal
- Make `collectEpicChildren` return epic tasks in dependency order so `ergo show <epic-id>` task order matches `ergo list`.

## Background / Rationale
- `tree_view.go` already relies on `topoSortTasks`; `collectEpicChildren` still uses lexicographic ID sort.
- Inconsistent ordering across commands creates operator confusion and can encourage incorrect execution sequence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- For sequenced tasks A→B→C within one epic, both `ergo show <epic-id>` (human) and `ergo show --json <epic-id>` list children as A, B, C.
- Tasks in the epic without dependency edges still appear exactly once.
- When multiple tasks share the same dependency depth, fallback order is deterministic (ID).
- Existing `ergo show <task-id>` behavior is unchanged.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run 'TestCollectEpicChildrenSortsByDependencyOrder'`
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run 'TestShowEpicChildrenDependencyOrder'`
- `go test ./...`

## Completion Notes
- Replaced `collectEpicChildren` ID sorting with `topoSortTasks(children, graph)` so show ordering now follows dependency order.
- Added `TestCollectEpicChildrenSortsByDependencyOrder` in `internal/ergo/commands_work_test.go` for command-layer ordering guarantees.
- Added `TestShowEpicChildrenDependencyOrder` in `cmd/ergo/integration_test.go` to verify both JSON and human show output order.
- Validated with targeted gates and full suite: `go test ./...`.

## Goal
- Redesign human `ergo show <epic-id>` output to be document-first: epic body first, compact task list second, dim metadata footer last.
- Keep `ergo show <task-id>` output unchanged.

## Background / Rationale
- Current epic show prints full task detail blocks for each child, which overwhelms the epic document and slows status scanning.
- A compact task list preserves execution context while keeping the plan document as the primary artifact.

## Layout Contract
```
# Epic Title
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<body, glamour-rendered when TTY>

Tasks  ·  <summary counts>
  <icon/state> <title + inline claim/blocker hints> ... <ID right-aligned>
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<EPICID>  ·  created <relative>  ·  updated <relative>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Order is: title heading → body (if non-empty) → compact task list → dim footer.
- Task list uses one row per child (no nested `printTaskDetails` output).
- Rows include existing state icon/color semantics plus inline claim and blocker hints.
- Summary counts are derived from existing stats helpers (or exact equivalent behavior).
- IDs remain right-aligned to match list output conventions.
- Empty epic body omits the body section cleanly (no extra blank artifact).
- `ergo show <task-id>` output remains unchanged.
- `ergo show --json <epic-id>` output remains unchanged.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run 'TestShowEpicHumanDocumentFirstLayout|TestShowEpicOmitsBodySectionWhenEmpty|TestShowTaskHumanOutputUnchanged'`
- `go test ./...`
- Manual: run `ergo show <epic-id>` against a mixed-state epic in both TTY and non-TTY contexts.

## Consult Me
- If preserving right-aligned IDs requires truncation/wrapping tradeoffs for long titles, pause and confirm the preferred behavior before finalizing.
- If you need to change glyphs, color semantics, or footer wording beyond this contract, confirm before merging.

## Completion Notes
- Added a dedicated epic renderer in `RunShow` so epic human output is now document-first: heading/body, compact task rows, and dim footer.
- Kept `printTaskDetails` path unchanged for `ergo show <task-id>` and preserved JSON show output shape.
- Reused existing list-view visual contracts by using `stateIcon`, `stateColor`, blocker logic, `formatTreeLine`, `computeStatsForTasks`, and `renderSummary` helpers.
- Added integration tests: `TestShowEpicHumanDocumentFirstLayout`, `TestShowEpicOmitsBodySectionWhenEmpty`, and `TestShowTaskHumanOutputUnchanged`.
- Validated with targeted gates and full suite: `go test ./...`.

## Goal
- Redesign human `ergo show <epic-id>` output to be document-first: epic body first, compact task list second, dim metadata footer last.
- Keep `ergo show <task-id>` output unchanged.

## Background / Rationale
- Current epic show prints full task detail blocks for each child, which overwhelms the epic document and slows status scanning.
- A compact task list preserves execution context while keeping the plan document as the primary artifact.

## Layout Contract
```
# Epic Title
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<body, glamour-rendered when TTY>

Tasks  ·  <summary counts>
  <icon/state> <title + inline claim/blocker hints> ... <ID right-aligned>
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<EPICID>  ·  created <relative>  ·  updated <relative>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Order is: title heading → body (if non-empty) → compact task list → dim footer.
- Task list uses one row per child (no nested `printTaskDetails` output).
- Rows include existing state icon/color semantics plus inline claim and blocker hints.
- Summary counts are derived from existing stats helpers (or exact equivalent behavior).
- IDs remain right-aligned to match list output conventions.
- Empty epic body omits the body section cleanly (no extra blank artifact).
- `ergo show <task-id>` output remains unchanged.
- `ergo show --json <epic-id>` output remains unchanged.
- `quickstart.txt` notes that `ergo --json show <epic-id>` returns full child task bodies, so agents reach for it instead of looping over `show` per task.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run 'TestShowEpicHumanDocumentFirstLayout|TestShowEpicOmitsBodySectionWhenEmpty|TestShowTaskHumanOutputUnchanged'`
- `go test ./...`
- Manual: run `ergo show <epic-id>` against a mixed-state epic in both TTY and non-TTY contexts.

## Consult Me
- If preserving right-aligned IDs requires truncation/wrapping tradeoffs for long titles, pause and confirm the preferred behavior before finalizing.
- If you need to change glyphs, color semantics, or footer wording beyond this contract, confirm before merging.

data.title: Document that show <epic> JSON includes child bodies
data.body:
## Goal
- Update `quickstart.txt` to document that `ergo --json show <epic-id>` returns full child task bodies, so agents use one call instead of looping `show` per task.

## Context
- Observed agent pattern in the wild: `for id in ...; do ergo --json show $id | python3 -c "extract title,body"; done`
- The data is already there — `ergo --json show <epic>` returns `{"epic": {...}, "children": [{...body...}, ...]}` — but agents do not know to reach for it.
- This is a discoverability fix, not a code change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent workflow section in `quickstart.txt` mentions that `ergo --json show <epic-id>` returns child task bodies.
- Wording is concise (1-2 lines) and placed where agents will see it during orientation.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...` passes (quickstart content is tested)
- Read the updated quickstart and confirm the new line is natural, not bolted on.

## Goal
- Redesign human `ergo show <epic-id>` output to be document-first: epic body first, compact task list second, dim metadata footer last.
- Keep `ergo show <task-id>` output unchanged.

## Background / Rationale
- Current epic show prints full task detail blocks for each child, which overwhelms the epic document and slows status scanning.
- A compact task list preserves execution context while keeping the plan document as the primary artifact.

## Layout Contract
```
# Epic Title
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<body, glamour-rendered when TTY>

Tasks  ·  <summary counts>
  <icon/state> <title + inline claim/blocker hints> ... <ID right-aligned>
──────────────────────────────────────────────────
<EPICID>  ·  created <relative>  ·  updated <relative>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Order is: title heading → body (if non-empty) → compact task list → dim footer.
- Task list uses one row per child (no nested `printTaskDetails` output).
- Rows include existing state icon/color semantics plus inline claim and blocker hints.
- Summary counts are derived from existing stats helpers (or exact equivalent behavior).
- IDs remain right-aligned to match list output conventions.
- Empty epic body omits the body section cleanly (no extra blank artifact).
- `ergo show <task-id>` output remains unchanged.
- `ergo show --json <epic-id>` output remains unchanged.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run 'TestShowEpicHumanDocumentFirstLayout|TestShowEpicOmitsBodySectionWhenEmpty|TestShowTaskHumanOutputUnchanged'`
- `go test ./...`
- Manual: run `ergo show <epic-id>` against a mixed-state epic in both TTY and non-TTY contexts.

## Consult Me
- If preserving right-aligned IDs requires truncation/wrapping tradeoffs for long titles, pause and confirm the preferred behavior before finalizing.
- If you need to change glyphs, color semantics, or footer wording beyond this contract, confirm before merging.

## Goal
- Update `quickstart.txt` to document that `ergo --json show <epic-id>` returns full child task bodies, so agents use one call instead of looping `show` per task.

## Context
- Observed agent pattern in the wild: `for id in ...; do ergo --json show $id | python3 -c "extract title,body"; done`
- The data is already there — `ergo --json show <epic>` returns `{"epic": {...}, "children": [{...body...}, ...]}` — but agents do not know to reach for it.
- This is a discoverability fix, not a code change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent workflow section in `quickstart.txt` mentions that `ergo --json show <epic-id>` returns child task bodies.
- Wording is concise (1-2 lines) and placed where agents will see it during orientation.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...` passes (quickstart content is tested)
- Read the updated quickstart and confirm the new line is natural, not bolted on.

## Completion Notes
- Added an explicit agent-workflow step in `internal/ergo/quickstart.txt` documenting that `ergo --json show EPICID` returns `epic` plus `children` with child `body` fields.
- Kept wording concise and placed it directly in the orientation loop where agents choose discovery commands.
- Validated with `go test ./...`.

data.title: Make ergo show Markdown-first
data.body:
## Goal
- Make `ergo show <task-id>` and `ergo show <epic-id>` emit a Markdown-legal, editor-friendly document by default (human output).
- Adopt SKILL.md-style front matter for the top of the document.
- For epics: show the epic body first, then include all child task bodies.

## Background / Rationale
- Feedback: epic `show` should be a document for review, not a status dashboard.
- Current/previous formats include terminal-oriented separators/icons and layout that is not a structured Markdown document in editors.
- Agents already have stable `--json` contracts; this change is for human-mode output only.

## Scope
- Human output only (`ergo show <id>` without `--json`).
- Apply to both task and epic show.
- Epic show includes all child task bodies.

## Non-goals
- No changes to `--json` output shape/fields.
- No new commands.

## Open Calls
- Confirm exact front matter keys (SKILL.md style, but minimal and stable).
- Confirm whether to keep relative timestamps in front matter or omit timestamps entirely.

data.title: Define SKILL.md-style show front matter
data.body:
## Goal
- Define the exact SKILL.md-style front matter format for `ergo show` human output for tasks and epics.

## Background / Rationale
- YAML front matter is widely used in Markdown workflows; adopting the SKILL.md convention makes `show` output predictable and editor-friendly.
- We need a stable set of keys so downstream scripts (if any) can rely on it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ergo show <task-id>` begins with front matter delimited by `---` lines.
- `ergo show <epic-id>` begins with front matter delimited by `---` lines.
- Front matter keys are documented in-task (short list) and are consistent between task and epic.
- No ANSI colors, box-drawing separators, or non-Markdown “UI layout” appear outside the task/epic bodies.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...`

## Consult Me
- Confirm which keys to include. Proposed minimal set:
  - Common: `kind`, `id`, `title`
  - Task-only: `state`, `epic_id`
  - Epic-only: `kind=epic`
  - Optional: `created_at`, `updated_at` (ISO8601) vs omit timestamps.

data.title: Make ergo show <task> a Markdown document
data.body:
## Goal
- Change human `ergo show <task-id>` output to be a Markdown document with SKILL.md-style front matter.

## Background / Rationale
- Today task show mixes terminal formatting concerns (separators, compact UI cues) with Markdown body rendering.
- We want the entire output to be consumable as Markdown in an editor.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is plain UTF-8 text with no ANSI color codes.
- Starts with SKILL.md-style front matter (per `IYKVXI` decisions).
- Uses Markdown structure:
  - `# <Task title>` heading
  - A small metadata section (Markdown bullets) as needed (id/state/epic)
  - Task body included verbatim after metadata
  - Results rendered as a Markdown list (path + summary)
- No blank “spacer” lines in the metadata section beyond standard Markdown paragraph breaks.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...`
- Add/adjust integration tests to assert:
  - front matter present
  - no separator lines
  - no ANSI escape sequences

## Consult Me
- If we need to preserve any legacy header line shape for backward compatibility, stop and confirm.

data.title: Make ergo show <epic> a Markdown document with child bodies
data.body:
## Goal
- Change human `ergo show <epic-id>` output to be a single Markdown document with SKILL.md-style front matter.
- Make the epic body the primary content.
- Include all child task bodies.

## Background / Rationale
- Original feedback: reviewing an epic is about reading the plan document; task status is secondary.
- We still want the epic show output to be self-contained for review, including child task bodies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is plain UTF-8 text with no ANSI codes.
- Starts with SKILL.md-style front matter (per `IYKVXI` decisions).
- Uses Markdown structure:
  - `# <Epic title>` heading
  - Epic body verbatim (if any)
  - `## Tasks` section
  - For each child task (dependency-ordered):
    - `### <TASKID> - <Task title>`
    - Include only minimal metadata: `state` and `results` (no claim, deps/rdeps, timestamps)
    - Include task body verbatim
- If epic body is empty, omit the epic body block cleanly.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...`
- Add/adjust integration tests to assert:
  - epic show includes child bodies
  - excludes claim/deps/rdeps/timestamps for child tasks
  - remains dependency-ordered

## Consult Me
- If showing child task bodies makes the output too long for common usage, confirm whether we should add a future flag rather than changing default.

data.title: Update help/quickstart to reflect Markdown-first show
data.body:
## Goal
- Update `internal/ergo/help.txt` and `internal/ergo/quickstart.txt` so the manual matches the new Markdown-first `show` behavior.

## Background / Rationale
- `help.txt` and `quickstart.txt` are the complete manual for agents/humans.
- Changing default show formatting must be reflected immediately to avoid doc drift.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ergo --help` text mentions that `show` outputs a Markdown document in human mode.
- `ergo quickstart` includes a short note and example snippet that shows front matter + headings.
- Explicitly restate that `--json` output is unchanged.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...`

## Goal
- Define the exact SKILL.md-style front matter format for `ergo show` human output for tasks and epics.

## Background / Rationale
- YAML front matter is widely used in Markdown workflows; adopting the SKILL.md convention makes `show` output predictable and editor-friendly.
- We need a stable set of keys so downstream scripts (if any) can rely on it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ergo show <task-id>` begins with front matter delimited by `---` lines.
- `ergo show <epic-id>` begins with front matter delimited by `---` lines.
- Front matter keys are documented in-task (short list) and are consistent between task and epic.
- No ANSI colors, box-drawing separators, or non-Markdown “UI layout” appear outside the task/epic bodies.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./...`

## Consult Me
- Confirm which keys to include. Proposed minimal set:
  - Common: `kind`, `id`, `title`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
  - Task-only: `state`, `epic_id`

## Completion Notes
- Implemented SKILL.md-style YAML front matter for human `show` output.
- Final key set is:
  - Task: `kind`, `id`, `title`, `state`, `epic_id`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
  - Epic: `kind`, `id`, `title`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
- Front matter values are YAML-safe quoted strings.

data.title: Implement ergo plan v1 (JSON document -> epic graph)
data.body:
## Goal
- Add a first-class `ergo plan` command that creates an epic + task dependency graph from one JSON document.
- Preserve existing CLI/event contracts while reducing multi-call planning overhead for agents.

## Scope (v1)
- New top-level command: `ergo plan`.
- Input: JSON object on stdin with epic fields + task array + `after` references by task title.
- Output: JSON object in `--json` mode and compact human summary otherwise.
- Validation: required fields, unknown keys, duplicate task titles, dangling `after`, cycle detection.
- Mutation semantics: single lock acquisition and transaction-style commit under lock for all-or-nothing durability.

## Non-goals (v1)
- `--body-stdin` mode for plan input.
- Flags-only plan authoring.
- `plan --update`/diff mode.
- Create-and-claim within plan.

## Invariants / Compatibility
- Reuse existing event types (`new_epic`, `new_task`, `link`) and dependency semantics (`from_id` depends on `to_id`).
- Keep existing commands and existing `--json` contracts backward compatible.
- Keep help/quickstart/spec fully accurate after introducing the new command.

## Risks to manage
- Partial-write risk during multi-event commit path.
- Validation drift between new plan parser and existing input/error conventions.
- Dependency-direction mistakes that invert readiness behavior.

data.title: Implement plan input schema and validation
data.body:
## Goal
- Define the v1 plan JSON contract in code (`title`, optional `body`, non-empty `tasks[]`, optional `after[]`).
- Add strict parse/validation behavior consistent with existing JSON mutation inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New plan input parser rejects malformed JSON, unknown keys, missing required fields, empty `tasks`, duplicate task titles, dangling `after` refs, and cycles.
- Unknown keys are surfaced as `parse_error` with fuzzy suggestions where applicable.
- Semantic validation failures are surfaced as `validation_failed` with actionable field-level detail.
- Validation does not mutate `.ergo` state.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run PlanInput`
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Parse.*Plan`

data.title: Implement plan create pipeline and event generation
data.body:
## Goal
- Build the core `ergo plan` mutation flow that turns validated input into one epic + N tasks + dependency edges.
- Preserve current dependency semantics (`from_id` depends on `to_id`) and reuse existing event types.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given valid input, plan creation generates one epic and all tasks in input order, assigns all tasks to that epic, and emits dependency links from `after` mappings.
- Uses a single lock acquisition and a single replay of current graph state per command invocation.
- IDs are generated internally and returned in command output; caller never threads IDs during creation.
- Any validation/mutation failure returns an error with no persisted partial graph.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run RunPlan`
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run PlanCreate`

data.title: Wire ergo plan command and output contracts
data.body:
## Goal
- Add top-level `ergo plan` Cobra command wiring and runtime invocation path.
- Implement human and JSON output contracts for successful plan creation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ergo --help` includes `plan` with succinct, accurate command text.
- `ergo --json plan` returns one JSON object containing epic metadata, ordered task list, and dependency edges (`from_id`, `to_id`, `type`).
- Human mode prints a compact one-line success summary with epic ID and counts.
- Command obeys existing global flags behavior (`--dir`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--verbose`).

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run Plan
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Output.*Plan

data.title: Add transaction-safe batch commit path for plan mutations
data.body:
## Goal
- Implement an internal commit path for multi-event writes that provides all-or-nothing durability under lock.
- Keep existing single-event append behavior untouched unless explicitly reused.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Batch commit writes full event log content via temp file + `fsync` + atomic rename under lock.
- On write failure, original event log remains valid and unchanged.
- Helper is documented and covered by focused storage tests for failure/rollback behavior.
- `ergo plan` uses this path for its multi-event mutation transaction.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Storage.*Plan
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Commit.*Atomic

data.title: Add comprehensive unit/integration coverage for ergo plan
data.body:
## Goal
- Add integration and behavior tests proving `ergo plan` works end-to-end and preserves existing readiness/dependency semantics.
- Cover invalid-input and no-partial-write failure paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration tests cover: happy path, duplicate titles, dangling refs, cycle detection, unknown keys, and malformed JSON.
- Tests assert dependency direction by checking readiness/blocking behavior after plan creation.
- Tests verify no entities are created when plan command fails validation.
- JSON output shape is asserted as a stable command contract.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./cmd/ergo -run Plan
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Plan

data.summary: Implemented plan JSON parsing + semantic validation

data.body:
## Goal
- Define the v1 plan JSON contract in code (`title`, optional `body`, non-empty `tasks[]`, optional `after[]`).
- Add strict parse/validation behavior consistent with existing JSON mutation inputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- New plan input parser rejects malformed JSON, unknown keys, missing required fields, empty `tasks`, duplicate task titles, dangling `after` refs, and cycles.
- Unknown keys are surfaced as `parse_error` with fuzzy suggestions where applicable.
- Semantic validation failures are surfaced as `validation_failed` with actionable field-level detail.
- Validation does not mutate `.ergo` state.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run PlanInput`
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Parse.*Plan`

## Completion Notes
- Added `PlanInput`/`PlanTaskInput` parsing and strict validation for required fields, duplicate titles, dangling `after` references, self-deps, and cycle detection.
- Added `ParsePlanInput` plus tests for unknown keys, malformed JSON, and multi-value stdin handling.

data.summary: Added atomic batch commit helper for multi-event writes

data.body:
## Goal
- Implement an internal commit path for multi-event writes that provides all-or-nothing durability under lock.
- Keep existing single-event append behavior untouched unless explicitly reused.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Batch commit writes full event log content via temp file + `fsync` + atomic rename under lock.
- On write failure, original event log remains valid and unchanged.
- Helper is documented and covered by focused storage tests for failure/rollback behavior.
- `ergo plan` uses this path for its multi-event mutation transaction.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Storage.*Plan`
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run Commit.*Atomic`

## Completion Notes
- Added transaction-safe event-log commit helpers (`replaceEventsAtomically`, `appendEventsAtomically`) using temp-file write, fsync, rename, and directory sync.
- Switched compact rewriting to use the shared atomic helper and added storage tests for append success and rollback on write failure.

data.summary: Implemented plan creation pipeline and event generation

data.body:
## Goal
- Build the core `ergo plan` mutation flow that turns validated input into one epic + N tasks + dependency edges.
- Preserve current dependency semantics (`from_id` depends on `to_id`) and reuse existing event types.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given valid input, plan creation generates one epic and all tasks in input order, assigns all tasks to that epic, and emits dependency links from `after` mappings.
- Uses a single lock acquisition and a single replay of current graph state per command invocation.
- IDs are generated internally and returned in command output; caller never threads IDs during creation.
- Any validation/mutation failure returns an error with no persisted partial graph.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run RunPlan`
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run PlanCreate`

## Completion Notes
- Implemented `RunPlan` to parse/validate payloads, generate epic/task IDs server-side, create dependency links from `after`, and persist all events in one atomic commit.
- Output contract now returns `kind=plan` with epic metadata, ordered tasks, and dependency edges matching existing `sequence` semantics.
